howItWorks.intro: Dovetail joints are the hallmark of quality woodworking. Proper spacing ensures even, attractive joints that are structurally sound. This calculator divides your board width into evenly-spaced pins and tails.
rules: Layout starts with half-pins at each edge, then alternates tails and full pins
Number of pins = number of tails + 1 (including two half-pins)
Dovetail angle is typically 1:6 for softwood and 1:8 for hardwood
Joint depth equals the board thickness (the mating board's thickness)
howItWorks.outro: Mark your dovetails from the narrow face (pin face). The tail board gets cut first, then used as a template to mark the pin board for a perfect fit. Leave your lines when cutting pins, split the line on tails.

faq.question: What dovetail ratio should I use?
faq.answer: Use 1:6 (about 14 degrees) for softwoods like pine, 1:7 for general purpose, and 1:8 (about 7 degrees) for hardwoods like oak and maple. Steeper angles (lower ratio) grip better in soft wood; shallower angles look more refined in hardwood.

faq.question: How many tails should I use?
faq.answer: A common guideline is one tail per inch of board width. A 6-inch board might have 3-4 tails. Fewer, larger tails look bolder; more, smaller tails look more refined. There's no structural difference.

faq.question: What are half-pins?
faq.answer: Half-pins are the triangular pieces at the top and bottom edges of the pin board. They prevent the joint from sliding apart and give a clean finished edge. They're typically 1/4 to 1/2 the width of a full tail.

faq.question: Should I cut pins first or tails first?
faq.answer: Both methods work. Tails-first is traditional for hand-cut dovetails — cut the tails, then use the tail board to mark the pins. Pins-first is preferred by some for through dovetails. Choose the method you're most accurate with.

faq.question: How tight should dovetails fit?
faq.answer: Aim for a fit that requires light mallet taps to assemble. Too tight and you'll split the wood; too loose and the joint is weak. The joint should go together with hand pressure and a few taps, not a hammer.

sections.title: What Is the Dovetail Calculator?
sections.body: The Dovetail Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your board width, number of tails, dovetail ratio, you get instant results including tail width, pin width, half-pin width. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting tail width right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your board width and need to find the right tail width. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Board Width (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your board width — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of tails, dovetail ratio, half-pin width, board thickness. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your tail width, pin width, half-pin width. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your board width before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both tail width and pin width — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your board thickness, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
